display.purpose: Upload-screen hero headline only — the single loudest moment in the product.

headline.purpose: Page-level titles: auth card title, history year markers.

title.purpose: Card and section headers (h2). Quiet — weight carries hierarchy, not size.

body.purpose: Transaction rows, form labels, descriptive copy. Generous line-height for long scan sessions.

label.purpose: Category badges, section eyebrows, pill text. The only place uppercase belongs.

shadows.name: ambient-float
shadows.purpose: Dropdown menus, statement pickers, chart tooltips — signals 'floating above the page'.

shadows.name: ambient-float-deep
shadows.purpose: Chart tooltips and the deepest floating surfaces.

shadows.name: accent-glow-resting
shadows.purpose: Primary buttons and submit actions at rest — a soft halo marking 'this is the action'.

shadows.name: accent-glow-active
shadows.purpose: Hover/focus state for primary actions — the glow widens rather than darkens.

shadows.name: accent-glow-wide
shadows.purpose: Upload dropzone hover state — the largest glow in the system, reserved for the single primary call to action on that screen.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default state transitions on borders, color, and background (0.12-0.18s).

motion.name: ease-shake
motion.purpose: Auth form error shake — the system's one expressive, non-standard easing, used sparingly for a single feedback moment.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Micro-interactions: pill hover, button active state.

motion.name: duration-standard
motion.purpose: Border color shifts, glow transitions, nav underline.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Submit / primary action button — gradient fill with a resting accent glow that widens on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #7c6fda, #9b8ee8); color: #ffffff; border: none; border-radius: 10px; padding: 0.6rem 1.5rem; font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 600; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 4px 18px rgba(124,111,218,0.2); transition: box-shadow 0.18s, transform 0.12s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { box-shadow: 0 0 22px rgba(124,111,218,0.2); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #7c6fda; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary navigation / utility action — flat surface fill with a hairline border that brightens on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: #0f0f1a; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border-radius: 8px; color: #8888aa; cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 500; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 0.38rem 0.85rem; transition: all 0.15s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #16162a; border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.14); color: #f0f0fa; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #7c6fda; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Content Card
components.description: The base container for tables, summaries, and panels — flat tonal surface with a hairline border that brightens on hover. No shadow at rest.
components.html:
Recurring charges

12 active subscriptions detected this month, totaling $186.40.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #0f0f1a; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border-radius: 16px; padding: 1.75rem; transition: border-color 0.15s; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card:hover { border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.14); } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #f0f0fa; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.65; color: #8888aa; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Filter and form fields — sits on the elevated plane, one tone brighter than its containing card, with a direct border-color focus shift (no glow, no ring).
components.css: .ds-input { background: #16162a; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.4rem 0.75rem; color: #f0f0fa; font-size: 0.85rem; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.15s; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #55556a; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: rgba(124,111,218,0.5); }

components.name: Category Pill
components.description: Inline category label on transaction rows — 15% tinted background of the category's reporting color with the full-saturation hue as text. One hue per category, never reused as chrome.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.2rem 0.6rem; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 0.71rem; font-weight: 600; white-space: nowrap; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-pill--groceries { background: rgba(0,200,83,0.15); color: #00c853; }

components.name: Filter Pill
components.description: Toggleable filter chip — surface fill with hairline border at rest; selected state shifts text to bright periwinkle and bolds, never changes the fill.
components.css: .ds-filter-pill { background: #0f0f1a; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07); border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.28rem 0.85rem; color: #8888aa; font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 500; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s; } .ds-filter-pill--active { border-color: rgba(124,111,218,0.45); color: #c4b8ff; font-weight: 600; } .ds-filter-pill:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #7c6fda; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Top Navigation
components.description: Primary nav link — flat text that brightens on hover with a violet underline that scales in from the left. The system's one piece of moving chrome, reserved for navigation alone.
components.html: DashboardTransactionsRecurring
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; gap: 1.5rem; align-items: center; } .ds-nav-link { background: none; border: none; color: #8888aa; cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 0.25rem 0; position: relative; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.15s; } .ds-nav-link::after { content: ''; position: absolute; bottom: -2px; left: 0; right: 0; height: 1.5px; background: #7c6fda; border-radius: 999px; transform: scaleX(0); transition: transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #f0f0fa; } .ds-nav-link:hover::after { transform: scaleX(1); }

components.name: Transaction Row (Hover Accent)
components.description: A borderless data-table row that brightens one tonal step on hover and reveals a violet edge on its leading cell — the Tonal Depth Rule applied to tabular data.
components.html: Jun 4Trader Joe'sUWCU Checking−$48.12
components.css: .ds-txn-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-txn-table td { padding: 0.6rem 0.5rem; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.07); font-size: 0.85rem; vertical-align: middle; transition: background 0.1s; } .ds-txn-row:hover td { background: #16162a; } .ds-txn-edge { border-left: 2px solid transparent; transition: border-color 0.1s, background 0.1s; } .ds-txn-row:hover .ds-txn-edge { border-left-color: #7c6fda; } .ds-txn-date { color: #55556a; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-txn-name { color: #f0f0fa; } .ds-txn-account { color: #55556a; font-size: 0.78rem; } .ds-txn-amount-cell { text-align: right; } .ds-txn-amount { font-weight: 600; color: #f87171; white-space: nowrap; }

narrative.overview: A finance tool that looks like it keeps a careful record, not one that's trying to sell you something. The surface sits in near-total darkness (#08080f) so the numbers, not the chrome, hold the user's attention during a long monthly review session. One muted violet (#7c6fda) carries every signal that matters: a focus ring, a primary action, a glow under a button. Everywhere else, the palette stays quiet, deep, and tonal, layered in three steps of near-black (base, surface, elevated) rather than dressed up with shadows or color blocking. This system explicitly rejects the gradient-hero-card, stat-tile SaaS template and the cartoon-bright consumer-budgeting look. There is no mascot energy here, no badges, no playful chart skins. Confidence comes from restraint: thin 1px borders that barely register until hover, type that holds its hierarchy through weight rather than ornament, and a single accent that earns attention by how rarely it competes for it.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black, three-step tonal surface stack (base → surface → elevated), no pure black and no warm neutrals
One accent color (muted violet, #7c6fda) doing all the signaling work: focus, primary actions, glow, links
Flat cards with hairline borders that only brighten on hover; depth implied by tone, not shadow
Type carries hierarchy through weight and negative letter-spacing on display sizes, not decoration
Category and status color exist only inside data visualizations (charts, pills, deltas) — never as page chrome

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Muted Violet is the only accent used for interface chrome (actions, focus, active state, glow). If a second 'brand' color appears outside of a chart or category pill, it's a mistake — route it back to violet or to a neutral.

rules.name: The Tonal Depth Rule
rules.body: Depth comes from moving one step up the Void → Surface → Elevated stack, never from drop shadows on resting elements. Shadows are reserved for things that visually float above the page (menus, modals, tooltips), at 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5-0.65).

rules.name: The Weight-Over-Size Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy is built primarily through font-weight steps (500 → 600 → 700/800) and negative letter-spacing on display sizes, not through a wide type scale. Most of the interface lives between 0.7rem and 1rem; only the upload hero and history year markers break past 1.7rem.

rules.name: The Float-Or-Flat Rule
rules.body: A surface either sits flush on the page (flat, tonal lift only) or visibly floats above it (ambient shadow). There is no in-between 'slightly raised card' — that's the lazy SaaS card-on-card look this system rejects.

dos: Do keep the page in the three-step tonal stack (#08080f → #0f0f1a → #16162a); every surface's depth should be readable from its background alone.

Do let Muted Violet (#7c6fda) be the only chrome accent — focus rings, primary actions, active nav, glows. If it shows up twice in the same view doing two different jobs, that's the system working as intended.
Do reserve saturated color (the reporting palette and CATEGORY_COLORS) for data: charts, pills, deltas, badges. These are the only places vivid hue belongs.

Do build hierarchy with font-weight (500/600/700/800) and tight letter-spacing on display sizes, keeping the type scale narrow (0.7rem-1.7rem covers nearly everything; 4rem is reserved for the single hero moment).
Do use the Float-Or-Flat rule: flat tonal cards at rest, soft ambient shadows (0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5-0.65)) only for things that visually float above the page.
donts: Don't add gradient hero cards, stat-tile templates, or 'enterprise analytics' dashboard chrome — PRODUCT.md names these directly as anti-references, and this system's whole point is that it doesn't look like it's selling anything.

Don't introduce gamified, consumer-budgeting-app energy: no badges-as-rewards, no cartoon chart skins, no mascot copy, no over-friendly micro-copy. The category pills and recurring badges are informational, not playful.

Don't use border-left/border-right colored stripes as static decorative accents on cards or list rows — every card uses a full hairline border; a stripe would be the one inconsistent shape in the system. The one exception is the recent-transactions table, where the leading cell's edge sits at 2px solid transparent and only brightens to violet on row hover/focus — that's the Tonal Depth Rule's brighten-on-interaction treatment applied to a table row, not a static stripe.
Don't add drop shadows to resting cards, or stack 'card on card' — depth comes from the tonal lift (Void → Surface → Elevated), never from shadow-on-flat-surface layering.
Don't introduce a second accent hue for interface chrome. If something needs to stand out, widen the violet glow or shift weight/contrast — don't reach for a new color.

Don't push the type scale past what's already here: no fonts beyond Inter, no display sizes beyond 4rem, no letter-spacing tighter than -2.5px. The narrow scale is what keeps a data-dense surface calm.
